e_visa.nationality: Non-African nationals subject to reciprocity principle
e_visa.notes: On 1 August 2025, Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko announced a mandatory e-visa (advance online prepayment required) for nationals of countries outside Africa that impose visa requirements on Senegalese citizens. Likely affected: France, USA, Canada, UK, China. The system was officially announced but implementation details were still pending as of June 2026. The portal visasenegal.sn returned HTTP 404 as of 2026-06-27 re-check. No official fee per applicant nor affected country list nor implementation date had been published on any .gouv.sn domain. ECOWAS citizens remain exempt. The existing Category A/B entry regime remains operative as of the validation date.

visa_policy.notes: Two operative tiers apply as of June 2026: Category A = fully visa-exempt for ordinary passport holders (ECOWAS members, most EU/EEA states, and bilateral-agreement countries); Category B = free visa on arrival at Dakar Blaise Diagne International Airport (DSS) only, up to 90 days. Category C = visa required before arrival. A reciprocity-based e-visa for non-African nationals was announced August 2025 but remains non-operational as of 2026-06-29 (portal visasenegal.sn returns HTTP 404). ECOWAS countries are all Category A. Several countries have split treatment: ordinary passport = Category B, diplomatic/service = Category A. Note: embassy page lists UAE in both A and B; this may be a page inconsistency - UAE retained in visa_free per Wikipedia corroboration but flagged in gaps. Embassy page also shows Chad and DRC in both A and B without qualifier; ordinary passport treatment for those is unconfirmed and noted in gaps. Vanuatu appears in both Category A and Category B on the embassy page; Category A retained per primary source listing, gap flagged.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of Algerian diplomatic and service passports are Category A (visa-exempt); ordinary passport holders require visa on arrival (Category B).

Holders of Brazilian diplomatic and service passports are also exempt from visa under bilateral agreement. NOTE: ordinary passport holders are now also Category A (visa-exempt) per 2026-06-29 re-verification of embassy page - see visa_free list. This conditional_access entry retained only to document the earlier EITI diplomatic/service record; the ordinary-passport-is-Category-C claim in the prior record was incorrect.

Holders of Chinese diplomatic, service, and official (passports for public affairs) passports are Category A (visa-exempt); ordinary passport holders require visa on arrival (Category B). Agreement entered into force 2014-05-03.
Holders of Gabonese diplomatic and service passports are Category A (visa-exempt); ordinary passport holders require visa on arrival (Category B).
Holders of Iranian diplomatic passports are exempt from visa for a stay of one month; ordinary passport holders require visa prior to entry (Category C).
Holders of Kenyan diplomatic and service passports are Category A (visa-exempt); ordinary passport holders are Category B (visa on arrival) per 2026-06-27 re-verification of embassy page.
Holders of Libyan diplomatic and service passports are Category A (visa-exempt); ordinary passport holders require visa on arrival (Category B).

Holders of Pakistani diplomatic, official, and service passports are exempt from visa under the Agreement on Visa Abolition for Holders of Diplomatic, Official and Service Passports signed between Pakistan and Senegal (approximately 2021; exact date not confirmed on official source).
Holders of South African diplomatic and service passports are Category A (visa-exempt); ordinary passport holders are Category B (visa on arrival) per 2026-06-27 re-verification of embassy page.
Holders of Tanzanian diplomatic and service passports are Category A (visa-exempt); ordinary passport holders require visa on arrival (Category B).
Holders of Turkish diplomatic passports are Category A (visa-exempt); ordinary passport holders require visa on arrival (Category B).
Holders of Ugandan diplomatic and service passports are Category A (visa-exempt); ordinary passport holders require visa on arrival (Category B).

cbi.notes: No official citizenship-by-investment programme found for Senegal. The investment promotion agency APIX (investinsenegal.sn) promotes foreign direct investment but does not offer a CBI or second-passport scheme.

validation_notes: 2026-06-29 VALIDATION: Re-fetched senegal-embassy.uk/list-of-countries-and-categories/ and confirmed the full Category A / B / C lists. visasenegal.sn e-visa portal confirmed still returning HTTP 404.

2026-06-29 CORRECTION: Brazil (BRA) ordinary passport added to visa_free (Category A). The embassy page lists Brazil in Category A with no passport-type qualifier. The prior record incorrectly classified Brazil ordinary passport as Category C, and only had Brazil in conditional_access for diplomatic/service passports. Wikipedia and third-party sources corroborate visa-free entry for Brazil ordinary passport holders. Conditional_access Brazil entry updated to remove the erroneous Category C claim.

2026-06-29 NOTE: Vanuatu appears in BOTH Category A and Category B on the embassy page (no passport qualifier). The current record retains Vanuatu in visa_free (Category A) as that is the higher/primary listing. Wikipedia lists Vanuatu under visa on arrival. This conflict is flagged in gaps - the true status requires confirmation against a .gouv.sn source.

2026-06-29 NOTE: E-visa reciprocity portal (visasenegal.sn) returned HTTP 404 again. Announced August 2025, still non-operational as of 2026-06-29. Policy announcement confirmed across multiple sources (VisasNews, APAnews, Travel and Tour World) but no official fee, country list, or launch date published. Affected countries likely include France, USA, Canada, UK, China per press reports.
2026-06-29 NOTE: diplomatie.gouv.sn/venir-au-senegal/ returned a Drupal database error - no visa policy content accessible from the official Senegal MFA domain.
2026-06-27 VALIDATION: Re-fetched senegal-embassy.uk/list-of-countries-and-categories/ and confirmed the full Category A / B / C lists.

2026-06-27 CORRECTION: Kenya ordinary passport moved from visa_free to visa_on_arrival. The embassy page explicitly lists 'Kenya (ordinary passport)' in Category B. The previous record had Kenya in Category A (visa_free) which was incorrect for ordinary passport holders. Diplomatic/service Kenya passports remain Category A in conditional_access.

2026-06-27 CORRECTION: South Africa ordinary passport moved from visa_free to visa_on_arrival. The embassy page lists 'South Africa' ordinary in Category B and 'South Africa (Diplomatic & Service passport)' in Category A. The previous record had South Africa in visa_free for ordinary passports which was incorrect.

2026-06-27 NOTE: UAE appears in both Category A and Category B on the embassy page. This is an apparent page inconsistency. Wikipedia corroborates Category A (visa-exempt) for UAE. UAE retained in visa_free with a note; ambiguity flagged in gaps.

2026-06-27 NOTE: Chad appears in both Category A and Category B on the embassy page, suggesting a diplomatic/ordinary split. Since Chad was already in visa_on_arrival (Category B) and the Category A listing is ambiguous (no passport-type qualifier given), Chad ordinary remains in visa_on_arrival; gap noted.

2026-06-27 NOTE: Solomon Islands appears in Category C on the embassy page. It is also listed in Category B as 'Salamon Islands' (misspelling). The existing visa_on_arrival record is retained per safety rules (cannot resolve the conflict definitively; category C listing adds risk of false visa-free). Flagged in gaps.

2026-06-27 NOTE: Republic of the Congo (COG) is not individually visible on the embassy page in the 2026 re-check. Wikipedia corroborates visa-exempt status. Entry preserved per safety rule (unconfirmable = keep as-is). Added note to entry.
2026-06-27 NOTE: E-visa reciprocity portal (visasenegal.sn) returned HTTP 404. System announced August 2025 remains non-operational as of this validation date. E-visa entry note updated accordingly.
2026-06-27 NOTE: diplomatie.gouv.sn loaded but showed no visa policy data on the homepage; SSL/content issue means no usable policy data from Senegal MFA domain.

HISTORICAL (prior run): ~30 countries previously misclassified as Category B were correctly moved to Category A (visa_free). ECOWAS members confirmed. France, UK, USA, Canada, India, Japan, Singapore, UAE, South Korea confirmed as Category A. E-visa system non-operational per prior validation.
HISTORICAL (prior run): conditional_access entries for Gabon, South Africa, Tanzania added for diplomatic/service split; redundant diplomatic entries for already-Category-A countries removed.
gaps: UAE appears in both Category A and Category B on the embassy page (senegal-embassy.uk); the correct tier for UAE ordinary passport is ambiguous. UAE retained in visa_free (Category A) based on Wikipedia corroboration but should be re-verified against a .gouv.sn source.

Vanuatu appears in both Category A and Category B on the embassy page without a passport-type qualifier. Current record retains Vanuatu in visa_free (Category A) but Wikipedia lists Vanuatu under visa on arrival. Requires confirmation against a .gouv.sn source.

Chad appears in both Category A and Category B on the embassy page without a passport-type qualifier for the Category A listing. Chad's ordinary passport treatment is unconfirmed. Chad retained in visa_on_arrival (Category B) pending clarification.

Solomon Islands: embassy page lists it in Category C ('Solomon Islands') and also has a misspelling 'Salamon Islands' in Category B. Wikipedia says visa on arrival. The conflict is unresolved; Solomon Islands retained in visa_on_arrival but Category C listing on the official embassy page is a risk flag.

Republic of the Congo (COG) was not individually identifiable on the embassy page during the 2026-06-27 and 2026-06-29 re-checks. Entry preserved from prior run per safety rules; requires independent verification.

Democratic Republic of the Congo (COD) appears in both Category A and B on the embassy page without passport-type distinction. The Category A listing may refer to diplomatic/service passports. No split conditional_access entry has been added pending confirmation; COD ordinary passport currently sits in visa_on_arrival.

The official e-visa portal (visasenegal.sn) returned HTTP 404 as of both 2026-06-27 and 2026-06-29; the reciprocity e-visa announced August 2025 remains non-operational. No official per-applicant fee, affected country list, or launch date has been published on any .gouv.sn domain.

diplomatie.gouv.sn and diplomatie.gouv.sn/venir-au-senegal/ showed no accessible visa policy content during the 2026-06-29 validation (Drupal database error); Ministry of Foreign Affairs visa policy cannot be independently verified from the official Senegal MFA domain.
Full Category C (visa required) country list is not individually enumerated in this record - only visa-free and visa-on-arrival entries are listed; Category C is the residual default.
Pakistan-Senegal diplomatic/service passport visa abolition agreement exact date not confirmed from official text; only Pakistan MFA press release available.
Russia and Serbia diplomatic/service passport waivers reported on Wikipedia but not confirmed from the official embassy page or EITI PDF; not included in conditional_access.
No official RBI (residence by investment / golden visa) programme was found or confirmed.
No official fast-track / digital nomad / startup visa programme was found.

outbound_official.note: No official Senegal government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Senegal's Ministry of African Integration and Foreign Affairs (diplomatie.gouv.sn) publishes only inbound visa rules (who may enter Senegal); no page listing destinations accessible to Senegalese ordinary-passport holders was found on any .gouv.sn domain. Consular sites cover only document issuance for Senegalese nationals abroad.

visa_types.name: Visa on Arrival (Court Séjour)
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay visit for tourism, family visits, or general purposes for Category B nationals. Issued by an immigration officer upon landing at Blaise Diagne International Airport (DSS).
visa_types.notes: Available only at Dakar Blaise Diagne International Airport (DSS). Requires valid passport, return air ticket, proof of accommodation (tourism) or Letter of Intent (business), and yellow fever vaccination card. Free of charge. Applies to Category B nationals only; Category A nationals are visa-exempt and do not need this.

visa_types.name: Special Business Authorisation (SBA)
visa_types.purpose: Formal authorisation for business activities in Senegal for nationals who do not qualify for standard Category A or B entry. Required for certain business visitors from restricted-access countries.
visa_types.notes: Downloadable SBA application form available from the Embassy of Senegal in London. Must be submitted to the relevant Senegalese embassy or consulate before travel. The embassy states all visa applications are free of charge.

visa_types.name: Prior Authorisation Visa (Visa avec autorisation préalable)
visa_types.purpose: Pre-travel visa authorisation required for Category C (restricted) nationals and holders of refugee travel documents (1951 Convention). Approval is granted by Dakar Central Visa Office or Ministry of Interior; the actual visa stamp is issued on arrival at DSS airport.
visa_types.notes: Two application routes: (1) Apply via a Senegalese embassy abroad - minimum 4 months processing. (2) Have a Senegal-resident contact apply at Dakar Central Visa Office on your behalf - 1 to 2 weeks minimum; requires phone interview with Ministry of Interior. Required documents: copy of travel document, letter of invitation with full contact details of inviter, passport photo. Upon approval, a short-stay visa is stamped in the passport at DSS airport. Applies to Category C nationals and refugee travel document holders. Free of charge.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic / Official Passport Visa Exemption
visa_types.purpose: Visa-free entry for holders of diplomatic, service, or official passports from countries with bilateral visa-abolition agreements with Senegal, even where the country's ordinary passport holders fall under Category B or C.
visa_types.notes: Countries with split treatment (ordinary = Category B or C, diplomatic/service = Category A visa-free) include: Algeria (diplomatic/service), China (diplomatic/service/official), Gabon (diplomatic/service), Iran (diplomatic - 30-day limit), Kenya (diplomatic/service), Libya (diplomatic/service), Pakistan (diplomatic/official/service), South Africa (diplomatic/service), Tanzania (diplomatic/service), Turkey (diplomatic), Uganda (diplomatic/service). Entry is granted directly without a stamp or prior application for diplomatic passport holders.
